rulings.Case Title: GARY YAEGER SR et al VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for September 1, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Campbell Industries' motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The deposition testimony of Gary Yaeger, Jr. precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that it is not liable under a premises liability theory. Defendant failed to demonstrate that the premises liability authority on which it relies bars plaintiffs' claims against it. Gary Yaeger, Jr.'s deposition testimony and plaintiffs' special interrogatory responses preclude defendant from sustaining its initial burden on this issue because they demonstrate that defendant's alleged liability is based on the affirmative conduct of defendant's employees, not on the theories discussed in defendant's authority. That is, even if defendant is correct that plaintiffs cannot demonstrate that defendant failed to warn of a concealed, pre-existing hazardous condition or negligently exercised control over decedent's work, defendant still would not demonstrate that plaintiffs cannot successfully assert their premise owner/contractor liability cause of action against it. (See Biles v. Exxon Mobil Corp. (2004) 124 Cal.App.4th 1315.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD POBLITZ et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for September 1, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Frederick Meiswinkel, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is continued to September 28, 2016 pursuant to the August 30, 2016 ex parte order. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD POBLITZ et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for September 1, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 2. Intervenors Maryland Casualty Company's and Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's, on behalf of Marconi Plastering Company, Inc., motion for summary judgment is off calendar at Intervenors' request. = (503/GLW)

If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
